title: COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT DISABILITIES PROGRAM - 211info
text: MARION COUNTY INTELLECTUAL AND DEVELOPMENTAL DISABILITIES COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT DISABILITIES PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a range of support services to individuals experiencing intellectual and/or developmental disabilities. Services may include case management, individual and family support plans, access to brokerage services for transportation, in-home support or residential placement, and employment and vocational services. 3180 Center Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Children and adults with an intellectual or developmental disability. Applicants must be an Oregon resident and US citizen. Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call to request an application for Developmental Disabilities services. An appointment will be scheduled with an eligibility worker who can help you through the application process. FEES: None DOCUMENTS: Proof of citizenship, proof of address, and records of any/all psychological and medical assessments related to the disability. AREA SERVED: Marion County Services: Benefits and Services Assistance * Disabilities Issues Case/Care Management * Disabilities Issues Disability Related Center Based Employment Disability Related Parenting Programs Disability Related Transportation Home/Community Based Developmental Disabilities Programs Residential Placement Services for People with Disabilities If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. MARION COUNTY INTELLECTUAL AND DEVELOPMENTAL DISABILITIES
